slides.tagline: [One-line brand statement — bold, compressed, unmistakable. e.g. 'WE SHOOT WHAT OTHERS MISS.']
slides.notes: Cover: high-contrast, near-black + red. The studio name should be typeset large in Barlow Condensed 800. The tagline sits beneath as a sharp one-liner. No explanations — let the visual command the room.

slides.headline: [Manifesto headline — compressed and provocative. e.g. 'IMAGE IS EVERYTHING. EVERYTHING IS IMAGE.']
slides.body: [2–3 lines expanding the manifesto. Should feel like a statement of belief, not a mission statement. First person or direct address — 'We believe…' or 'Your brand deserves…']
slides.notes: Manifesto slide uses full-bleed dark background. One dominant headline (Barlow Condensed 800) plus 2–3 lines of body copy (Barlow 300/400). No bullet points. The copy should feel hand-written, not committee-approved.

slides.studio_label: [STUDIO TYPE — e.g. CREATIVE STUDIO / PHOTO AGENCY / VISUAL STUDIO]
slides.body: [One powerful paragraph: who you are, what you specialize in, what drives you. Write as if speaking directly to your ideal client. Avoid generic agency language. Be specific about your niche.]
slides.notes: About slide: minimal text, maximum character. Show a hero image crop or studio visual if available. List 4–6 capabilities in ALL CAPS — this is a stylistic choice for the Hhart Red template.

slides.headline: [Project name or campaign title — in Hhart Red's compressed bold style]
slides.description: [One sentence about the brief and what you delivered. e.g. 'Full campaign identity and hero imagery for global product launch — 12 markets, 3 continents.']
slides.visual_hint: [Describe the hero visual: bold photography, high-contrast graphic, campaign frame. Red/dark palette preferred.]
slides.notes: Work slides: go full-bleed visual. The image is the hero. Text should be minimal — client, year, category, and one line of context. Let the work speak. If you have multiple strong images from this project, consider a grid.

slides.headline: [Project name or campaign title]
slides.description: [One sentence about the brief and outcome. Show range — different industry or visual style from Work 01.]
slides.visual_hint: [Describe the visual approach — different tone from Work 01 to show versatility]
slides.notes: Second work slide: prove range. If Work 01 was high-contrast dark, try something with more texture or editorial feel here. Same minimal text approach — the visual carries the weight.

Numbers slide: use Barlow Condensed 800 for the values, Barlow 300 uppercase for the labels. Red accent on one standout number. No explanations — the numbers should be self-evident or intriguing enough to spark conversation.

slides.title: HOW WE WORK

steps.label: DISCOVER
steps.desc: [What this phase involves — 1 sentence max. e.g. 'Brief analysis, moodboarding, and competitive landscape. We understand your world before we build in it.']

steps.label: DEFINE
steps.desc: [e.g. 'Creative direction, visual language, and shot list. No surprises on shoot day.']

steps.label: DELIVER
steps.desc: [e.g. 'Final assets, usage rights, and brand guidelines. We hand over everything you need.']

slides.notes: Process slide for Hhart Red should feel like a system, not a flowchart. Use the four D's or your own process language. Keep descriptions to one sentence each — the spare copy style reinforces the bold visual identity.

slides.quote: [Client quote — ideally specific and visual. e.g. 'They turned a brief into a universe. Every image we received felt like it already existed — we just hadn't found it yet.']
slides.author: [Client Name]
slides.notes: Testimonial slide: red background or high-contrast treatment. The quote is the only text — no supporting graphics. Pick a quote that feels as bold as the visual work. Generic praise doesn't belong in this template.

slides.title: [Closing line — a call to action that matches the studio's attitude. e.g. 'YOUR STORY. OUR LENS.' or 'READY TO MAKE SOMETHING UNFORGETTABLE?']
slides.notes: CTA slide: back to the cover visual language — dark, bold, red accent. End with the same confidence you opened with. The contact details should be easy to screenshot. If presenting live, stay on this slide during the follow-up conversation.
